Content: Cambell: Hello? Schafer: Hello!
Content_zh: Cambell：喂？Schafer：你好！

Content: Cambell: It's open.
Content_zh: Cambell：門沒鎖。

Content: Oh, god.
Content_zh: 天啊

Content: How many flights of these stairs are there?
Content_zh: 這樓梯到底有幾層啊？

Content: [Heavy breathing] [Coughs]
Content_zh: [沉重的呼吸] [咳嗽]

Content: [Gasping] So out of shape.
Content_zh: [喘氣] 體力也太差了。

Content: Gonna die.
Content_zh: 要死了

Content: [Deep breaths] [Knocking]
Content_zh: [深呼吸] [敲門]

Content: [Wheezing] Cambell: Hi, dude. Schafer: Hi.
Content_zh: [喘鳴] Cambell：嗨，老兄。Schafer：嗨。

Content: Scott Campbell. Cambell: You made it up the stairs.
Content_zh: Scott Campbell。Cambell：你真的爬上來了。

Content: Schafer: Do you wanna work on our adventure game?
Content_zh: Schafer：你想來做我們的冒險遊戲嗎？

Content: Cambell: Yes. Schafer: Okay.
Content_zh: Cambell：好。Schafer：好。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Ah, you wanna come to the stand up?
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：你要來站會嗎？

Content: Crook: Come and do our stand up. We're gonna have it right here.
Content_zh: Crook：來參加我們的站會吧。我們就在這裡開。

Content: Cambell: You guys stand up for the whole thing?
Content_zh: Cambell：你們為整件事站起來？

Content: Schafer: Come to our stand up. Cambell: Okay.
Content_zh: Schafer：來我們的站會吧。Cambell：好啊。

Content: I kind of have to use the...
Content_zh: 我得用……

Content: you know... Schafer: Nope.
Content_zh: 你懂的…… Schafer：不行。

Content: Cambell: ...you-know-what first. Schafer: Nope.
Content_zh: Cambell：……先去一下那個。Schafer：不行。

Content: Oh yeah?
Content_zh: 是嗎？

Content: We were doing stand ups when you were here.
Content_zh: 你以前在這裡時我們就有站會了。

Content: Kipnis: Ah, Scott, holy shit.
Content_zh: Kipnis：啊，Scott，靠。

Content: Hey! Cambell: Hey!
Content_zh: 嘿！Cambell：嘿！

Content: Kipnis: Hey! Cambell: How are you?
Content_zh: Kipnis：嘿！Cambell：你好嗎？

Content: Is this how you guys always do it, just standing in the middle?
Content_zh: 你們都這樣開嗎？就站在中間？

Content: Schafer: You stand in the middle and then you close your eyes and you lean and we pass the body around.
Content_zh: Schafer：你站在中間，閉上眼睛往後倒，然後我們把你傳來傳去。

Content: Rice: Good, how's it goin'? Cambell: Good, man.
Content_zh: Rice：很好，你呢？Cambell：不錯，老兄。

Content: Schafer: You know Brandon? Dillon: No, we haven't met before.
Content_zh: Schafer：你認識 Brandon 嗎？Dillon：不，我們之前沒見過。

Content: Schafer: Brandon, Scott Campbell. Cambell: Hey man.
Content_zh: Schafer：Brandon，Scott Campbell。Cambell：嘿，老兄。

Content: Skillman: Oh man, we're being filmed. Man, this is so awkward, but it's nice to see you.
Content_zh: Skillman：天啊，我們被拍了。真尷尬，不過很高興見到你。

Content: McGarry: Oh, it's on camera. What's up, buddy?
Content_zh: McGarry：喔，在拍啊。最近怎樣，兄弟？

Content: Good to see you.
Content_zh: 很高興見到你

Content: Hackett: That's all we do around here is hug.
Content_zh: Hackett：我們這裡整天都在抱抱。

Content: Cambell: Hey, buddy. Hug sounds good, man.
Content_zh: Cambell：嘿，兄弟。擁抱聽起來不錯。

Content: Muir: I never met Peter Chan in person.
Content_zh: Muir：我從來沒親眼見過 Peter Chan。

Content: McGarry: I never met him either. Crook: Really?
Content_zh: McGarry：我也從沒見過他。Crook：真的？

Content: [Murmuring] Schafer: Bap-bee-dee-bap-bup-ba-da, buh-bum, ba-da-dum-dum-dum.
Content_zh: [低語聲] Schafer：Bap-bee-dee-bap-bup-ba-da，buh-bum，ba-da-dum-dum-dum。

Content: Ya-da-za-ha.
Content_zh: Ya-da-za-ha。

Content: Let's see, are we all here? Everyone here?
Content_zh: 我看看，人都到了嗎？大家都在嗎？

Content: Hello, hello, everybody.
Content_zh: 大家好，大家好。

Content: Hi, thanks for coming.
Content_zh: 嗨，謝謝大家過來。

Content: Uh, please join me in welcoming some special guests we have here today.
Content_zh: 請大家跟我一起歡迎今天的幾位特別來賓。

Content: We have from--some out-of-towners.
Content_zh: 有幾位是從外地來的。

Content: We have Mr. Peter Chan over here, Peter Chan.
Content_zh: 這邊有 Peter Chan 先生，Peter Chan。

Content: Hello.
Content_zh: 你好

Content: [applause] Uh, we have Mr. Scott Campbell. Where's Scott Campbell?
Content_zh: [掌聲] 還有 Scott Campbell 先生。Scott Campbell 在哪？

Content: There's Scott Campbell! Yay, Scott Campbell.
Content_zh: Scott Campbell 在那裡！耶，Scott Campbell。

Content: [Applause] And we have, uh, Nathan "Bagel" Stapley.
Content_zh: [掌聲] 還有 Nathan "Bagel" Stapley。

Content: There he is.
Content_zh: 他在那裡。

Content: [Applause] Anyone else coming in from out of town?
Content_zh: [掌聲] 還有誰是從外地來的嗎？

Content: Is anyone here? Great.
Content_zh: 還有人嗎？很好。

Content: Um, we are doing, um, uh, an art jam for Reds.
Content_zh: 嗯，我們正在替 Reds 辦一場美術集思會。

Content: Reds is the code name for our Double Fine Adventure.
Content_zh: Reds 是我們《Double Fine Adventure!》的代號。

Content: This last week, we did one of my favorite things, which was the art jam, which we got all our artists from across the country
Content_zh: 上週我們做了我最喜歡的事之一，就是美術集思會，把全國各地的美術都找來

Content: here in this room sitting around one table just drawing and talking and pitching ideas back and forth.
Content_zh: 聚在這個房間同一張桌子旁，一邊畫、一邊聊，互相拋想法。

Content: And we had Peter Chan who has been working with me since "Monkey Island 2."
Content_zh: 我們找來了 Peter Chan，他從《Monkey Island 2》就跟我一起工作。

Content: We have Scott Campbell, who's here since the beginning of Double Fine.
Content_zh: 還有 Scott Campbell，他從 Double Fine 創立初期就在這裡。

Content: And Nathan "Bagel" Stapley, who, um, whose art is gonna be featured front and center
Content_zh: 還有 Nathan "Bagel" Stapley，他的美術會是這次的核心。

Content: in the Double Fine Adventure, and who has also been here since the beginning of Double Fine.
Content_zh: 會放在《Double Fine Adventure!》最顯眼的位置，而且他也是 Double Fine 初期就在的人。

Content: It's good to see you guys, I haven't seen, you know, a lot of you in the office for a while, so it's awesome to have you here.
Content_zh: 很高興見到你們。我已經好一陣子沒在辦公室看到你們了，所以你們能來真的很棒。

Content: Um, people who don't know who these guys are, ask somebody.
Content_zh: 如果有人不知道他們是誰，去問旁邊的人。

Content: [Laughter] Cambell: You know, I'm excited. It's like a total reunion.
Content_zh: [笑聲] Cambell：我很興奮。這根本像大團圓。

Content: The last time we were all together was, um, beginning of "Brütal Legend." Peter was--Peter's been on, I think, every one of his projects.
Content_zh: 上次我們全員聚在一起，是《Brütal Legend》剛開始的時候。Peter……我想 Tim 每個專案都有 Peter。

Content: Chan: Here that, uh, hired me. Right here.
Content_zh: Chan：就是這位，呃，當初雇用我的人。就在這裡。

Content: Gilbert: I hired more people in this industry than you know.
Content_zh: Gilbert：我在這行雇過的人比你想像的還多。

Content: Chan: Tim and everyone was going, "We would like you to come back and help us out on this."
Content_zh: Chan：Tim 和大家都說：「我們希望你回來幫我們做這個。」

Content: And that was the friendliest you've seen Ron right there, fellas.
Content_zh: 各位，那大概就是你們看過 Ron 最親切的樣子了。

Content: To collaborate with, uh, these guys Ron and Tim again is, you know, is like the good old days, you know?
Content_zh: 能再跟 Ron 和 Tim 這些人合作，就像回到以前的好時光。

Content: So I didn't want to pass that up.
Content_zh: 所以我不想錯過這個機會。

Content: Schafer: Art is obviously really critical to graphic adventures.
Content_zh: Schafer：美術顯然對圖像冒險遊戲非常關鍵。

Content: It's part of the defining thing of like, "Let's have an adventure game, but let's add not just text to it.
Content_zh: 它的核心概念有一部分就是：「我們來做冒險遊戲，但不要只有文字。」

Content: Let's actually show the world." And seeing beautiful worlds laid out in front of you
Content_zh: 「我們真的把世界呈現出來。」看到美麗的世界展現在眼前，

Content: that inspire your imagination.
Content_zh: 會激發你的想像力。

Content: Your desire to explore them is such an important thing, 'cause they don't have, um, you know,
Content_zh: 你探索它們的渴望是件很重要的事 因為它們沒有……

Content: an RPG's progression drive, you know, or an addictive hook like collectables or something like that usually.
Content_zh: 通常不像 RPG 那樣有升級推進力，也沒有收集品那種讓人上癮的誘因。

Content: It's just the--the thing that pulls you forward and keeps you going is the-- the extent to which we've ignited your desire to explore.
Content_zh: 真正把你往前拉、讓你繼續玩下去的，就是我們能在多大程度上點燃你探索的慾望。

Content: So I was gonna go over the state of the game like storywise and what design there is on the game,
Content_zh: 所以我想先講一下遊戲目前的狀態，像是故事進度，以及現在有了哪些設計，

Content: and then talk about, uh, art jam, too, a little bit, 'cause this is a little-- this is a little earlier than I think we did--
Content_zh: 然後也稍微談一下美術集思會，因為這次比我印象中以前做的還要早一點……

Content: Like, when Peter--by the time Peter visited on "Brütal," we already had a bunch of art that we then kind of bundled up
Content_zh: 像 Peter 來參與《Brütal》的時候，我們已經有一堆美術素材，後來把它們整理起來

Content: and made the factions and stuff like that.
Content_zh: 做成各個陣營之類的東西。

Content: This is, I think, a little earlier in the process.
Content_zh: 我覺得這次是在流程更早期一點。

Content: I was hoping to-- I asked Greg to kind of get you guys here as soon as possible to help generate ideas while I'm designing the game,
Content_zh: 我希望……我請 Greg 盡快把你們找來，在我設計遊戲時一起激發點子，

Content: 'cause, um, the game could go so many different areas.
Content_zh: 因為這款遊戲可以往很多不同方向發展。

Content: And I have this idea about the boy and the girl, but, you know, there's the spaceship, but there could be anything on the spaceship,
Content_zh: 我已經有男孩和女孩的概念，但你知道，太空船在那裡，而太空船上可以有任何東西，

Content: and there's this pastoral world, but it could be any level of-- it could have anything in it right now, and I think a lot of these, I think coming up
Content_zh: 還有這個田園世界，但它現在可以往任何方向發展，裡面也可以放任何東西。我覺得很多這類點子，

Content: with just visual ideas for cool places to go are just as valid a place to start as anything.
Content_zh: 光是先想出一些很酷、值得去的地方的視覺概念，也跟任何起點一樣可行。

Content: Petty: This one is, we're at such an early state on, uh, Reds, that, um, you know, Tim-- Tim's only really identified
Content_zh: Petty：這次 Reds 還在非常早期的階段，所以 Tim……Tim 其實只確認了

Content: a few very specific things for us.
Content_zh: 幾個很明確的東西給我們。

Content: Schafer: Right now, the green cards are characters-- these are the characters so far--
Content_zh: Schafer：現在綠色卡片是角色，這些是目前為止的角色。

Content: and the yellow cards are just story points.
Content_zh: 黃色卡片只是故事節點。

Content: Um, I'm gonna be adding locations here and, um, puzzles in different colors.
Content_zh: 我會在這裡加上地點，還有用不同顏色標出的謎題。

Content: I mean, I told them a lot about what we have.
Content_zh: 我是說，我跟他們講了很多目前已經有的東西。

Content: That's really as far as the game has gone because, um, I've been doing a lot of business stuff, which is annoying.
Content_zh: 遊戲目前真的就只到這個程度，因為我一直在處理很多商務上的事，很煩。

Content: Yeah, so I'm way behind on the design and the puzzles, but we did hire a business guy.
Content_zh: 所以我在設計和謎題上落後很多，不過我們確實雇了一位商務人員。

Content: Rice: So that's good.
Content_zh: Rice：那很好

Content: Schafer: Which means I won't have to work on Hausen anymore.
Content_zh: Schafer：也就是說我不用再處理那些事了。

Content: But luckily, we just hired a business guy to come in and take care of that stuff.
Content_zh: 幸好我們剛雇了一位商務人員進來處理那些事。

Content: Trying to get Brad's new project signed, and, uh, there were a lot of meetings at E3 and stuff.
Content_zh: 像是努力讓 Brad 的新專案簽下來，還有 E3 一堆會議之類的。

Content: Um, you know, there's a few more details that only make sense to me, but, uh, um,
Content_zh: 還有一些只有我自己看得懂的細節，不過，呃……

Content: I mostly gave those guys the heads up about where-- where everything is right now, yeah.
Content_zh: 我主要是先讓他們知道目前所有東西進展到哪裡。

Content: Uh, it's a story about a boy and a girl, and they both have two different parallel stories going on,
Content_zh: 這是關於一個男孩和一個女孩的故事，他們各自有兩條平行進行的故事線，

Content: and the player can jump back and forth between them.
Content_zh: 玩家可以在兩邊來回切換。

Content: And the girl is, um, a young girl who's been chosen for this big honor that she finds out is actually being sacrificed
Content_zh: 女孩是個年輕女孩，被選中接受一項莫大的榮耀，但她後來發現那其實是要把她獻祭

Content: to a horrible monster in order to save her town.
Content_zh: 給可怕的怪物，好拯救她的城鎮。

Content: And her story is about choosing to break out of that arrangement and endanger her town
Content_zh: 她的故事就是選擇打破這個安排，即使可能讓自己的城鎮陷入危險，

Content: and hopefully fight the monster and save her town.
Content_zh: 並希望能對抗怪物、拯救城鎮。

Content: And then the boy is a, um-- has grown up alone on a spaceship, a giant, kind of organic spaceship
Content_zh: 男孩則是……獨自在一艘太空船上長大，那是一艘巨大、有點像有機體的太空船，

Content: that takes care of his every needs and creates entertainment for him and takes care of his food and his health and everything,
Content_zh: 會照顧他的所有需求，替他安排娛樂，照顧他的飲食、健康和一切。

Content: but he's all by himself out in space.
Content_zh: 但他在太空中孤單一人。

Content: And it's about him actually finding out, um, how to take control of that ship
Content_zh: 他的故事則是發現自己其實可以掌控那艘船，

Content: and get access to new secret areas and find out that he has the ability to actually, uh,
Content_zh: 進入新的秘密區域，並發現自己其實有能力……

Content: have a higher purpose than just hanging out in this ship being entertained,
Content_zh: 追求比待在船上被娛樂更高的目的，

Content: but he can actually go on rescue missions and save helpless creatures around the galaxy.
Content_zh: 他其實可以出任救援任務，拯救銀河各處無助的生物。

Content: Chan: Like all the other adventure games, I think he had more landmarks already established.
Content_zh: Chan：像以前那些冒險遊戲，我覺得他已經先建立了比較多支柱。

Content: More tent poles, right?
Content_zh: 比較多核心支柱，對吧？

Content: And, um, and then my job was to connect between them.
Content_zh: 然後我的工作就是把那些支柱串起來。

Content: And this time around, yeah, there's less tent poles, and so it's our job to go in and try to figure out
Content_zh: 這一次支柱比較少，所以我們的工作就是進去試著找出

Content: what the other places are right now.
Content_zh: 其他地方現在會是什麼樣子。

Content: Basically, all we're doing is giving him enough of inspiration for him to run with it.
Content_zh: 基本上，我們做的就是給他足夠的靈感，讓他可以接著發揮。

Content: Once he sees an image and his imagination starts, you know, uh, walking around in our drawings,
Content_zh: 只要他看到一張圖，想像力開始在我們的畫裡四處遊走，

Content: he can then start to expand on that.
Content_zh: 他就能從那裡開始延伸。

Content: That's how it always works with Tim.
Content_zh: Tim 一向都是這樣。

Content: Petty: On a creative direction standpoint, you know, Tim--I think Tim fishes a lot, you know?
Content_zh: Petty：從創意方向的角度來看，Tim……我覺得 Tim 很常在撒網找東西。

Content: And, um, he kind of gets these elements and then he makes unusual but cohesive connections
Content_zh: 他會拿到這些元素，然後在元素之間做出不尋常但很有整體感的連結，

Content: between those elements, you know?
Content_zh: 你懂我的意思吧？

Content: And I think that this will help that process for him.
Content_zh: 我覺得這會幫助他完成那個過程。

Content: Other people maybe have-- have in mind a very specific mythology, and I think Tim's different in different games.
Content_zh: 有些人腦中可能會有一套很明確的神話架構，而我覺得 Tim 在不同遊戲裡方法都不太一樣。

Content: Like, I think "Brütal Legend," you know, he had a very, like, "It's a roadie in this heavy metal fantasy world,
Content_zh: 像《Brütal Legend》，他有一個很明確的概念：「這是一個重金屬奇幻世界裡的巡演技師，

Content: and it's based on the look of album covers," and Frazetta--some of the very early stuff.
Content_zh: 視覺基礎是唱片封面，」還有 Frazetta，那些很早期的東西。

Content: A little more--a little more specific than some of this, but I think in the end, he just takes different ways to get there
Content_zh: 那比這次某些東西更明確一點，但我覺得到頭來，他只是會用不同方式抵達目標，

Content: depending on what he feels the game needs or what the theme of the game is really about.
Content_zh: 取決於他覺得遊戲需要什麼，或遊戲主題真正想談的是什麼。

Content: Schafer: But, um, it's still in the phase where just, like, everything is like, "Yes, and that could be cool and this could be cool
Content_zh: Schafer：但它現在還在那種階段，所有東西都是：「對，那可能很酷，這個也可能很酷，

Content: and this could be cool," and just like, coming up with tons of art.
Content_zh: 這個也可能很酷。」然後就是產出大量美術。

Content: That's my goal. All right?
Content_zh: 這是我的目標 好嗎？

Content: Petty: There's these big gaps in the first two acts past the intro, and that's where I think we can help Tim the most.
Content_zh: Petty：前兩幕在開場之後還有很大的空白，我覺得那正是我們最能幫上 Tim 的地方。

Content: [Music] Schafer: It is--I'm joking, but it is like a dream team.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：我是開玩笑的，但這真的像夢幻團隊。

Content: I mean, these are the best artists I know, and they're all here together.
Content_zh: 我是說，這些都是我認識最棒的藝術家，而且他們全都聚在這裡。

Content: [Music] Cambell: Man, these are a little dry, dude.
Content_zh: [音樂] Cambell：老兄，這些有點乾了。

Content: Chan: Who makes this? Petty: Copic.
Content_zh: Chan：這是誰出的？Petty：Copic。

Content: These are Japanese markers and they're the best, because first, they got a square shape so they don't roll on your table.
Content_zh: 這些是日本麥克筆，超好用。首先它們是方形的，所以不會在桌上滾來滾去。

Content: Right, and they got nice-- nice medium-sized tip, a very nice chisel tip.
Content_zh: 對，而且筆頭很棒，是很好的中型斜角筆頭。

Content: And they--and they really can lay it down, so all you do to refill it is you just pull this sucker off like that.
Content_zh: 而且它們很能上色。要補充墨水時，只要像這樣把這傢伙拔下來。

Content: And you can buy just tip replacements, too.
Content_zh: 也可以只買替換筆頭。

Content: -Yeah. -Oh yeah.
It's like, uh, I met some-- I met Chris.
Content_zh: 就像，呃，我遇到了一些…… 我遇到了克里斯。

Content: Cambell: Like a steak--it's like a meat-- it's like a bacon steak root.
Content_zh: Cambell：就像牛排…… 就像肉……

Content: -Very nice. -One down.
Content_zh: -很不錯。-完成一個。

Content: Bagel: Yam. -Who else?
Content_zh: Bagel：山藥。-還有誰？

Content: Chan: So she starts exploring.
Content_zh: Chan：所以她開始探索

Content: When she reaches, like, pathways which are made of twigs, all right?
Content_zh: 等她走到那種用樹枝做成的小路時，對吧？

Content: Schafer: Mm-hm. Chan: So there are some paths.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯哼。Chan：所以那裡有一些路徑。

Content: Once she's on it, she won't sink anymore, so that--that automatically guides her through.
Content_zh: 她一走上去就不會再下沉，所以那會自動引導她通過。

Content: Schafer: Textural. Chan: Now--awesome.
Content_zh: Schafer：很有質感。Chan：現在……很棒。

Content: Petty: When he wasn't looking, or when they needed to operate the ship, they started to like fold back, and they have all these like,
Content_zh: Petty：當他沒在看，或是他們需要操作飛船時，它們就會開始往後翻開，裡面有一堆像是

Content: um, like A.M. clock radio innards, and the arms come out and they start doing stuff
Content_zh: 像 AM 時鐘收音機的內部構造，手臂會伸出來開始做事

Content: and he turns around and they're like back as a little bunny.
Content_zh: 等他轉過身，它們又變回小兔子的樣子。

Content: Cambell: So, like, these people would be cruisin' around on like combs and brushes, dude, and they'd be combing the land like farming.
Content_zh: Cambell：所以這些人會騎著梳子和刷子到處跑，老兄，像耕田一樣梳理土地。

Content: So it'd be like farming style, but they'd be farming the trees and shit.
Content_zh: 所以會有點像農耕風格，只是他們耕的是樹啊之類的。

Content: Schafer: Are they pulling out lice to eat or anything?
Content_zh: Schafer：他們會拔出蝨子來吃之類的嗎？

Content: Cambell: No, it'd just be really pretty.
Content_zh: Cambell：不，它會非常漂亮。

Content: That's the thing, they can be farming, harvesting all the bugs and shit, and then they could put it in their little harvester,
Content_zh: 就是這樣，他們可以耕作、收成所有小蟲之類的東西，再放進他們的小收割機，

Content: but also making really cool shapes from the stuff that they create like that, you know what I mean?
Content_zh: 也從他們創造的東西 造出很酷的形狀，你知道我的意思嗎？

Content: Chan: So, that's what we've got.
Content_zh: Chan：所以我們目前就是這些。

Content: Bagel: Yeah, I think we're getting closer to-- I think we're just trying to figure out like a language because-- since these things are gonna be 2D painting,
Content_zh: Bagel：對，我覺得我們越來越接近了。我們只是想找出一套語言，因為這些東西會是 2D 繪圖，

Content: I think we really just have to figure out the language of both worlds.
Content_zh: 我覺得我們真的得找出兩個世界各自的視覺語言。

Content: And I think we're like getting...
Content_zh: 我覺得我們正在……

Content: I think all these drawings, once we put them all up tomorrow and see what Tim says,
Content_zh: 我覺得這些畫明天全部貼上去之後，再看 Tim 怎麼說，

Content: it'll even be just that next level of direction, you know?
Content_zh: 就會再往下一個方向前進一層，你懂嗎？

Content: Cambell: But he just gets worried when you throw away your drawings.
Content_zh: Cambell：但你把畫丟掉時，他就會很緊張。

Content: I don't know if you saw that today, but we had some--some run-ins with some of the artists throwing away their work
Content_zh: 不知道你今天有沒有看到，我們有幾次撞見一些藝術家把自己的作品丟掉，

Content: 'cause they don't think it's good enough.
Content_zh: 因為他們覺得不夠好

Content: Schafer: Don't let me bother you, I'm just looking at what you're drawing.
Content_zh: Schafer：別管我，我只是看看你在畫什麼。

Content: Those are pretty.
Content_zh: 真漂亮

Content: Where'd that squid go? Bagel: Squid?
Content_zh: 那隻烏賊去哪了？Bagel：烏賊？

Content: Petty: You were painting a squid earlier.
Content_zh: Petty：你剛纔在畫烏賊

Content: Bagel: Oh yeah, I threw it out, I threw it away.
Content_zh: Bagel：喔對，我丟了，把它丟掉了。

Content: Schafer: You threw it out? Petty: Oh!
Content_zh: Schafer：你把它丟了？Petty：喔！

Content: Schafer: You son of a-- Petty: We all liked it.
Content_zh: Schafer：你這個…… Petty：我們都很喜歡耶。

Content: Cambell: Why'd you throw that away, dude?
Content_zh: Cambell：老兄，你幹嘛把它丟掉？

Content: Bagel: Son of a monkey. Which one?
Content_zh: Bagel：猴崽子。哪一張？

Content: Petty: Get a whole sheet. Schafer: Oh my God, and you tore it in half?
Content_zh: Petty：拿一整張紙。Schafer：天啊，你還把它撕成兩半？

Content: Bagel: I can make another one.
Content_zh: Bagel：我可以再做一個

Content: Bagel: We'll make another one.
Content_zh: Bagel：我們再畫一張。

Content: Cambell: Dude, that whole fuckin' page.
Content_zh: Cambell：老兄，那可是整張該死的紙耶。

Content: Bagel: You guys liked it?
Content_zh: Bagel：你們喜歡嗎？

Content: Schafer: Yeah, we paid for that, we paid for that.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，我們可是有付錢的，我們付了錢。

Content: You pretty much just stole from the backers by tearing that up.
Content_zh: 你把它撕掉，基本上就是在偷贊助者的東西。

Content: Bagel: Yeah. Cambell: Why'd you rip that up?
Content_zh: Bagel：是啊。Cambell：你為什麼把它撕掉？

Content: -You crazy, man? Bagel: I know.
Content_zh: -你瘋了嗎？Bagel：我知道。

Content: Cambell: That was the best shit, man.
Content_zh: Cambell：那張超讚的耶，老兄。

Content: Bagel: Now, uh-- I didn't think it was that great.
Content_zh: Bagel：呃，我不覺得它有那麼好。

Content: Yeah, he picked it out of the trash, my drawing.
Content_zh: 對，他把我的畫從垃圾桶裡撿回來。

Content: And then he told me to do it over again.
Content_zh: 然後叫我再畫一次。

Content: Cambell: He'll probably Frankenstein that one and that'll be the...
Content_zh: Cambell：他大概會把那張拼拼湊湊，然後變成……

Content: Bagel: Oh, speak of the devil. Cambell: ...the biggest idea.
Content_zh: Bagel：喔，說曹操曹操到。Cambell：……最重要的點子。

Content: Schafer: You guys talking about something?
Content_zh: Schafer：你們在說什麼？

Content: That's cool.
Content_zh: 那很酷。

Content: Weird porcupine guys.
Content_zh: 奇怪的豪豬人。

Content: Bagel: It's like moving, uh, on land. Schafer: That's awesome.
Content_zh: Bagel：就像在陸地上移動。Schafer：太棒了。

Content: Chan: I have a lot of faith in Tim, and all we have to do is plant a few seeds
Content_zh: Chan：我對 Tim 很有信心，我們只要撒下幾顆種子，

Content: and he'll start running with it, right?
Content_zh: 他就會接著發揮，對吧？

Content: Cambell: Yeah, planting seeds.
Content_zh: Cambell：是啊，播種。

Content: Yeah, you don't know what seeds will turn into rad stuff, man.
Content_zh: 對啊，你不知道哪些種子最後會長成超酷的東西，老兄。

Content: [Music] Cambell: So it's just a list of possible room ideas and experiences, you know?
Content_zh: [音樂] Cambell：所以這只是一份可能的房間點子和體驗清單，你懂嗎？

Content: Just kind of--like, some of them are just words.
Content_zh: 有點像……有些就只是幾個詞。

Content: They may make us think about some interesting things, you know?
Content_zh: 它們也許會讓我們想到一些有趣的東西。

Content: Like wild jungle. It's fun for people.
Content_zh: 像是野性叢林，大家會覺得好玩。

Content: A sunny beach, candy room, garden zone for, like, the tender side of boys.
Content_zh: 陽光海灘、糖果房、花園區，給男孩比較溫柔的那一面。

Content: [Laughing] Maybe those seeds will grow into amazing things
Content_zh: [笑] 也許那些種子會長成神奇的東西

Content: and then we'll realize that those amazing things are actually terrible.
Content_zh: 然後我們會意識到 那些令人驚訝的事情其實是可怕的。

Content: So we'll just cover them up with a sheet and then move on to the next thing.
Content_zh: 所以我們就拿布把它們蓋起來，繼續做下一個東西。

Content: Who knows?
Content_zh: 誰知道呢？

Content: You know, fireball, soccer, playin' with fireballs are really fun, probably, for boys.
Content_zh: 像火球、足球，玩火球對男孩來說大概會很有趣。

Content: Laser zone--anything can be made into lasers, so you can eat sandwiches of lasers,
Content_zh: 雷射區，任何東西都可以變成雷射，所以你可以吃雷射三明治，

Content: you could just shoot 'em, ride 'em, dodge 'em. You know.
Content_zh: 你可以射它們、騎它們、閃它們，你懂的。

Content: I'll just do drawings and try to get people pumped.
Content_zh: 我就負責畫圖，試著讓大家嗨起來。

Content: It's my--That'll be my role.
Content_zh: 那就是我的角色。

Content: So this is like, just, could be waterfalls and different things of goop, and it could be falling down,
Content_zh: 所以這裡可能是瀑布，或各種黏糊糊的東西往下流，

Content: or this could be barf, you know?
Content_zh: 也可能是嘔吐物，你懂吧？

Content: And it could be like animal, or, you know, creatures made of barf kind of unhappy or happy, you know?
Content_zh: 可以是動物，或是用嘔吐物做成的生物，有點不開心或很開心之類的。

Content: Like, and they can be barfing new creatures.
Content_zh: 像是牠們可以吐出新的生物。

Content: It could just be barf zone, you know what I mean?
Content_zh: 也可以直接是嘔吐區，你懂我的意思吧？

Content: Schafer: Was there a period in your life where you had a barf thing?
Content_zh: Schafer：你人生中有一段特別迷嘔吐物的時期嗎？

Content: Cambell: People love barf, dude.
Content_zh: Cambell：大家都愛嘔吐物啦，老兄。

Content: Schafer: Oh, no, they don't.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔，不，他們才不愛。

Content: Cambell: At this point, everything is disposable and stuff, so.
Content_zh: Cambell：在這個階段，所有東西都可以丟掉之類的，所以……

Content: That's the thing about creative processes, like, you wanna go down every road fully
Content_zh: 創作過程就是這樣，你會想把每一條路都徹底走一遍，

Content: to realize that road is wrong.
Content_zh: 才會發現那條路是錯的。

Content: You can't just go down a little bit and be, "Ooh, uh..." You gotta really test that water fully
Content_zh: 你不能只走一小段就說：「喔，呃……」你得真的把水溫試到底，

Content: before you can realize that's a terrible road.
Content_zh: 才會知道那是一條爛路。

Content: And then you go down the next road.
Content_zh: 然後再走下一條路。

Content: Rice: This is a list of locations from our backers on the forums.
Content_zh: Rice：這是我們在論壇上贊助者的地點清單。

Content: Petty: You know, some of the stuff, we're just incorporating backer-- uh, backer ideas, which was cool.
Content_zh: Petty：有些東西我們其實就是把贊助者的點子納進來，這很酷。

Content: You know, but they don't really know anything about the story, which is kind of cool at this phase, right,
Content_zh: 但他們其實不太知道故事內容，而這個階段這樣反而滿酷的，對吧？

Content: that they don't really have to, that maybe some of those things will wind up working in ways.
Content_zh: 他們其實也不需要知道，也許其中一些東西最後會以某種方式派上用場。

Content: Rice: I went through the first ten pages so far.
Content_zh: Rice：我目前看完前十頁了。

Content: Schafer: Oh my God, how many pages are there?
Content_zh: Schafer：天啊，總共有幾頁？

Content: Rice: I know, there's 38 or something like that.
Content_zh: Rice：我知道，大概有 38 頁之類的。

Content: Um, we're still in girl world.
Content_zh: 我們還在女孩世界。

Content: Sand castle world?
Content_zh: 沙城堡世界？

Content: That's a new one. Bagel: Sand castles.
Content_zh: 這是新的。Bagel：沙堡。

Content: Rice: Sand castle world.
Content_zh: Rice：沙城堡世界。

Content: Boy world.
Content_zh: 男孩世界。

Content: A haunted lighthouse on the moon.
Content_zh: 月亮上的鬼燈塔

Content: Schafer: What? Bagel: I didn't see that moon comin'.
Content_zh: Schafer：什麼？Bagel：我沒想到會冒出月亮。

Content: Rice: Um, this dude says, "Inside of a computer." This dude says, "An entire city made of blanket forts."
Content_zh: Rice：這傢伙說 "電腦裡" 這傢伙說 "整個城市都是用毯子堡壘做的"

Content: That was something. Cambell: Yeah.
Content_zh: 那還真有意思。Cambell：對啊。

Content: Rice: MS-DOS world, where everything's made from words.
Content_zh: Rice：MS-DOS 世界，所有東西都是文字做的。

Content: Schafer: That's a text adventure, that's called a text adventure.
Content_zh: Schafer：這是文字冒險 這叫文字冒險

Content: Rice: "It is a place where you play connect-the-dots to build the location." Cambell: Fuckin' creative people, dude.
Content_zh: Rice：「那是一個用連連看來建造場景的地方。」Cambell：這些人他媽的真有創意，老兄。

Content: Rice: Yeah. Cambell: It's okay if we use that shit?
Content_zh: Rice：是啊。Cambell：我們用這些點子沒問題嗎？

Content: I mean, dude, we're gonna get...
Content_zh: 我的意思是，老兄，我們要去……

Content: Schafer: Yeah. Cambell: ...sued.
Content_zh: Schafer：沒問題。Cambell：……被告。

Content: Seriously, man, fuck, like, I'm so excited to see these lists.
Content_zh: 說真的，老兄，靠，我看到這些清單超興奮。

Content: I feel like--I feel like it's cheating, kind of.
Content_zh: 我覺得……我覺得這有點像作弊。

Content: Bagel: It's like, uh, they're stoked.
Content_zh: Bagel：就像，呃，他們很興奮。

Content: Like, if somebody gets their idea made, like, that'd be awesome.
Content_zh: 如果有人的點子真的被做出來，那會超棒。

Content: Cambell: And there's all these sequels based on that one idea that someone said, then they're gonna start being like, "Hm, maybe I should have held onto that idea."
Content_zh: Cambell：如果後來有一堆續集都建立在某個人說的點子上，他們就會開始想：「嗯，也許我當初該把那個點子留著。」

Content: Schafer: The hair trees, wanna mention that.
Content_zh: Schafer：那些頭髮樹，我想提一下。

Content: I love all these guys and that whole story, like, whatever-- I don't even know what it is yet, but like,
Content_zh: 我喜歡這些傢伙和那整個故事，不管它是什麼。我甚至還不知道那是什麼，但像是

Content: the idea of going into this, like, grass-covered thing that comes to life.
Content_zh: 走進這個覆滿草、會活過來的東西，這個概念很棒。

Content: And this behavior is really cool, it's just like, there's something lazy about it that I like.
Content_zh: 而且這種行為真的很酷，有種慵懶的感覺，我很喜歡。

Content: Cambell: We've just been trying to get as much stuff as we can.
Content_zh: Cambell：我們只是盡量收集越多東西越好。

Content: All right, with the big--I just think it's a round hunchback of forest right here.
Content_zh: 好，這個大……我覺得這裡就是一團圓圓駝背的森林。

Content: Schafer: It's kind of like those, uh, you know, those places in New Mexico or...
Content_zh: Schafer：就像那些，呃，你知道，那些地方 在新墨西哥州或……

Content: Cambell: We're not getting too into the mechanics of how the ship creates things or how the monster land looks or how the city looks,
Content_zh: Cambell：我們不會太深入研究飛船怎麼創造東西，或怪物之地、城市到底長什麼樣，

Content: but just kind of overall things that might spark stuff for him right now.
Content_zh: 而是先做一些整體上可能激發他靈感的東西。

Content: So we'll just do more of that tomorrow.
Content_zh: 所以我們明天再做更多

Content: [Music] Chan: I just, uh, did a lot of drawing on my own,
Content_zh: [音樂] Chan：我就是自己畫了很多圖，

Content: you know, self-taught.
Content_zh: 你知道，自學的。

Content: I would draw--when I was little, I would draw on paper, and then, if I didn't like something,
Content_zh: 我小時候會畫畫 畫在紙上 然後 如果我不喜歡什麼

Content: I would crumple it up and throw it away.
Content_zh: 我就會把它揉成一團丟掉。

Content: And I remember my father said to me, um, "Now, if you-- if you just slide your drawings into the garbage can,
Content_zh: 我記得我父親對我說：「如果你把畫直接平放進垃圾桶，

Content: and without crumpling it-- without crumpling it up, you would fit more drawings in the, um, garbage bin,"
Content_zh: 不要揉成一團，這樣垃圾桶裡就能放更多畫。」

Content: not knowing that he was going through it later, um, and pulling out the ones he liked, because, um, after he passed away,
Content_zh: 我當時不知道他後來會去翻垃圾桶，把他喜歡的挑出來。因為他過世後，

Content: I found a folder that was labeled, uh, "Peter," and I found my old Star Wars drawings.
Content_zh: 我找到一個標著「Peter」的資料夾，裡面有我以前畫的《Star Wars》圖。

Content: Uh, if he didn't do that, I wouldn't have any drawings, because I don't-- I don't really keep my--my stuff around.
Content_zh: 如果他沒那麼做，我現在一張畫都不會留下來，因為我其實不太留自己的東西。

Content: [Music] As you can see, uh, I tucked in the woods here,
Content_zh: [音樂] 如你所見，我這裡藏在樹林裡，

Content: and this is where I get my inspiration, is, uh, nature.
Content_zh: 而我靈感的來源就是大自然。

Content: Um, so, as you know, uh, you're on San Juan Island, and, uh, this is my little studio away from my home,
Content_zh: 你們現在在 San Juan Island，這是我離家不遠的小工作室，

Content: and this is where I create, uh, drawings for games and films.
Content_zh: 這就是我為遊戲和電影畫畫的地方

Content: I'm, uh, in here eight hours a day, and what's nice is on a beautiful day like today,
Content_zh: 我一天大概在這裡待 8 小時，而好處是，像今天這麼漂亮的日子，

Content: I--I, uh, I like to draw outside when I can.
Content_zh: 只要可以，我喜歡到外面畫。

Content: When I'm having a creative block, that's the time to go out, you know, go on walks and hikes,
Content_zh: 當我遇到創作瓶頸時，就是該出去走走、健行的時候，

Content: and that's important to be creative, too.
Content_zh: 這對創意也很重要

Content: I need to create that space to think.
Content_zh: 我需要建立思考的空間。

Content: I love it, can't go back.
Content_zh: 我很喜歡，已經回不去了。

Content: This fits and feels really, really good for my creative self and for my emotional self and everything else.
Content_zh: 這裡真的很適合我，對我的創作狀態、情緒狀態和其他一切都非常好。

Content: It's really good.
Content_zh: 真的很好。

Content: "Grim" actually was my last adventure game, and it was the-- and it was done here on the island.
Content_zh: 《Grim Fandango》其實是我上一款冒險遊戲，而且是在這座島上完成的。

Content: It was my first job away from Lucas.
Content_zh: 那是我離開 LucasArts 後的第一份工作。

Content: This was my goin' away present, uh, from Lucas, as you can see.
Content_zh: 這是我離開時 LucasArts 送我的禮物，如你所見。

Content: Never forget where you started, where you come from, and that's something really important to me.
Content_zh: 永遠別忘記自己從哪裡開始、從哪裡來，這對我非常重要。

Content: 'Cause that's why, um, I have, like, some Star Wars stuff in here.
Content_zh: 所以我這裡才會放一些《Star Wars》的東西。

Content: It's important that I have a few of those things around because that's what inspired me.
Content_zh: 身邊放幾樣這種東西很重要，因為它們就是啟發我的東西。

Content: That's what got me started.
Content_zh: 是它們讓我走上這條路。

Content: That's why this piece here, by Ralph McQuarrie, is so important to me,
Content_zh: 所以這幅 Ralph McQuarrie 的作品對我這麼重要，

Content: 'Cause he's the man that, um, inspired me to do what I'm doing now.
Content_zh: 因為就是他啟發我去做現在正在做的事。

Content: When I was ten, I didn't know that there was such thing as, a, you know, concept designer,
Content_zh: 我十歲時還不知道有所謂的概念設計師，

Content: someone that actually designs something for you to live in for those couple hours,
Content_zh: 有人會真的設計出一個世界，讓你在裡面待上幾個小時。

Content: if it was a movie, or--or now, hours playing video games.
Content_zh: 如果是電影就是幾個小時，現在玩電玩則可能是好幾個小時。

Content: I mean, it's a--an opportunity to escape and live in that world.
Content_zh: 我是說，那是一個逃離現實、住進那個世界的機會。

Content: This is where I draw, mostly.
Content_zh: 我主要就在這裡畫圖。

Content: I do everything standing up, and I still draw on paper, as you can see.
Content_zh: 我什麼都站著做，而且如你所見，我現在還是在紙上畫。

Content: Down here is, uh, all the bad ideas right here.
Content_zh: 下面這裡就是所有爛點子。

Content: See, these are the workups.
Content_zh: 你看，這些是草稿。

Content: From this came this one, where it's-- as you can see, is a total mess, and, uh, I try to come up with, uh, some funny ideas,
Content_zh: 這一張是從這裡發展出來的。如你所見，它一團亂，我會試著想出一些有趣的點子，

Content: and then from there, becomes this one up here.
Content_zh: 然後再從那裡變成上面這張。

Content: And then eventually, I use all those drawings, um, to start my fire in the morning.
Content_zh: 最後，我會用那些畫在早上生火。

Content: It's a good lesson on letting go.
Content_zh: 這是學會放手的好課。

Content: [Music] I'm gonna show you guys something.
Content_zh: [音樂] 我要給你們看點東西

Content: These are my family photo albums of when Tim and Pete Tscale came to visit,
Content_zh: 這些是我的家庭相簿，裡面是 Tim 和 Pete Tscale 來拜訪時的照片，

Content: and, uh, used to do work jams on the island with me at the, uh, farmhouse.
Content_zh: 以前會來島上的農舍，跟我一起做工作集思會。

Content: That's, uh, me in my upstairs loft studio out of-- in the farmhouse.
Content_zh: 那是我，在農舍樓上的閣樓工作室裡。

Content: And those are all the storyboards for "Grim Fandango." And here's me drawing.
Content_zh: 那些全都是《Grim Fandango》的分鏡。這是我在畫圖。

Content_zh: 我不記得了，但 Tim 堅稱我當時畫太快，連筆尖的小滾珠都射出去了。

Content: Is he gonna kill me? I gotta show you this.
Content_zh: 他會殺了我嗎？我一定要給你看這個。

Content: I'm not gonna show you the second one, 'cause I know he'll kill me for sure,
Content_zh: 我不會給你們看第二張，因為我知道他一定會殺了我，

Content: but I'm gonna give you this one because he looks so good in it.
Content_zh: 但我可以給你們看這張，因為他在裡面看起來很帥。

Content: Tim chopping wood.
Content_zh: Tim 砍柴。

Content: And I'm not gonna show you the second one.
Content_zh: 我不會給你看第二張

Content: That's a before, and then this one's after.
Content_zh: 那是之前的，然後這個是之後的。

Content: "Grim" is my favorite, um, adventure game that I've ever worked on.
Content_zh: 《Grim Fandango》是我做過最喜歡的冒險遊戲。

Content: But who knows, this could be the next favorite game.
Content_zh: 但誰知道呢，這款也許會成為下一個最愛。

Content: When I start a project, I start collecting my books.
Content_zh: 當我開始一個專案，我開始收集我的書。

Content: It's important to go and do your research, do it the old-fashioned way first.
Content_zh: 先用老派的方法做研究很重要。

Content: And I learn more by going to the library and reading up on it instead of just looking for a pretty picture off the internet.
Content_zh: 我去圖書館閱讀相關資料，學到的比只在網路上找漂亮圖片還多。

Content: And so for us to create our own village, I think it's important that we, um,
Content_zh: 所以我們要建立自己的村莊 我覺得我們……

Content: try to find that happy balance between what we're familiar with as fairytale--
Content_zh: 要試著在我們熟悉的童話元素之間找到恰到好處的平衡，

Content: the signature shape is, like, mushrooms, for, like, Alice in Wonderland--
Content_zh: 像《Alice in Wonderland》裡那種招牌的蘑菇形狀，

Content: and you balance it with something foreign, something that we're not familiar with.
Content_zh: 再用某種異國、我們不熟悉的東西來平衡它。

Content: In Japanese architecture, you can kind of see there's mushrooms in it without even thinking about it.
Content_zh: 在日本建築裡，你幾乎不用想就能看出裡面有蘑菇的形狀。

Content: There are these great mushroom shapes that are happening, and so I thought I'd mix that up with,
Content_zh: 裡面有很棒的蘑菇形狀，所以我想把它和

Content: um, Scandinavian, like country homes, and that brings in some of the really cute stuff
Content_zh: 斯堪地那維亞的鄉村住宅混在一起，這會帶進一些很可愛的元素，

Content: that's happening, see, with the thatched roofing.
Content_zh: 你看，像這種茅草屋頂。

Content: Here you can kind of see that my wood-burning stove that I put in the background here
Content_zh: 這裡可以看到我放在背景裡的柴爐，

Content: looks like one of these lanterns.
Content_zh: 看起來像這些燈籠之一。

Content: It has, like, the mushroom top.
Content_zh: 它有那種蘑菇狀的頂。

Content: And so, I'm just figuring that out right now, and then eventually have to pitch this to, um, Tim and Lee,
Content_zh: 所以我現在還在摸索，之後終究得把這些點子提給 Tim 和 Lee，

Content: and hopefully sell them the ideas that I have.
Content_zh: 希望能說服他們接受我的想法。

Content: Um, yeah, I can't wait.
Content_zh: 我等不及了

Content: And then what's nice, though, is that I'm able to shut that door at the end of the day and walk across the lawn and go home,
Content_zh: 但最美好的是 我終能關上門 走過草坪回家

Content: and I leave, uh, this game in the studio and it doesn't follow me-- follow me home,
Content_zh: 把這款遊戲留在工作室，不讓它跟著我回家，

Content: because my life at home is important.
Content_zh: 因為我在家的生活很重要

Content: And, uh, and actually, you know, we should get out of here and spend some time outside.
Content_zh: 其實我們應該離開這裡，到外面待一會兒。

Content: That's where some of the better ideas come from.
Content_zh: 有些更好的點子就是從那裡來的。

Content: [Music] Cambell: My name's Scott Campbell and I live here in New York City.
Content_zh: [音樂] Cambell：我叫 Scott Campbell，住在紐約市。

Content: We are in my apartment, which also is my studio.
Content_zh: 我們現在在我的公寓，這裡也是我的工作室。

Content: I'm an illustrator and I've worked at Double Fine, uh-- I think I was employee number six in 2001,
Content_zh: 我是插畫家，也曾在 Double Fine 工作。呃……我想我是 2001 年的第六號員工，

Content: and I was art director on "Psychonauts" and "Brütal Legend."
Content_zh: 我曾經是《Psychonauts》和《Brütal Legend》的藝術總監。

Content: Production designer for "Brütal Legend," my official title.
Content_zh: 《Brütal Legend》的製作設計師，這是我的正式職稱。

Content: When I was working at the studio, like, I liked to go into his office and just kind of show him,
Content_zh: 當我在工作室工作的時候，我喜歡去他的辦公室 給他看看

Content: like, uh, the paintings and drawings and stuff, and I would be able to gauge from his--
Content_zh: 像是那些畫作、草圖之類的，我就能從他的……

Content: his reaction whether or not it was good.
Content_zh: 他的反應判斷那東西好不好。

Content: That's what I miss living here in New York.
Content_zh: 這是我住在紐約時會懷念的事。

Content: One thing I know that Tim likes, though-- he likes drawings when they're in cool poses.
Content_zh: 不過我知道 Tim 有一點很喜歡，他喜歡角色擺出很酷姿勢的圖。

Content: Even if the designs aren't very good, he just likes poses.
Content_zh: 即使設計不好 他也喜歡姿勢

Content: So if there's a dude that I really like the design of, I'll put him in the coolest pose and hope that he digs it.
Content_zh: 所以如果我很喜歡某個角色設計，就會讓他擺最酷的姿勢，希望 Tim 會喜歡。

Content: It was one of my favorite poses is putting his-- putting your feet up on something.
Content_zh: 我最喜歡的姿勢之一就是把腳翹在某個東西上。

Content: Always sell Tim on this pose.
Content_zh: 這個姿勢總能說服 Tim。

Content: I haven't talked to Tim since the very beginning, so I don't know how it's evolved, you know?
Content_zh: 我從最一開始之後就沒再跟 Tim 談過，所以不知道它後來怎麼發展。

Content: Just when Tim first pitched the game to Bagel and I, he came here to New York.
Content_zh: Tim 第一次向 Bagel 和我提這款遊戲時，他來到紐約。

Content: We had some, uh, some sangria and some tacos and we talked about it and he gave us the pitch.
Content_zh: 我們喝了點桑格利亞、吃了些塔可，聊了這件事，他也向我們提案。

Content: And so after that, you know, Bagel--Bagel and I just kind of started just gathering our friends
Content_zh: 之後 Bagel……Bagel 和我就開始找朋友們一起來，

Content: and just trying out drawing the main two characters and some basic environment ideas.
Content_zh: 試圖畫出兩個主角和一些基本環境想法

Content: Pre-production is like when you get started and you start dream-- dream the dreams, you know?
Content_zh: 前期製作就像剛開始時，你開始做夢，做那些夢，懂嗎？

Content: It can be anything you want and stuff, and you're just kind of gathering inspiration.
Content_zh: 它可以變成任何你想要的樣子，而你就是在收集靈感。

Content: Gathering reference is like-- it's like my favorite part, so I'm constantly taking stuff from the internet,
Content_zh: 收集參考資料就像…… 這就像我最喜歡的部分 所以我經常從網路上拿東西

Content: like gathering images and stuff.
Content_zh: 像是收集圖片之類的。

Content: It's just like if ever I hit-- if ever I see something cool, I'll just kind of take it and put it in a folder for later.
Content_zh: 只要我碰到……只要看到很酷的東西，就會把它存起來，先放進資料夾裡。

Content: Just like kind of the effects or the colors, I don't know what it would inspire, but it just makes me feel pretty good.
Content_zh: 可能只是某種效果或顏色，我不知道它會激發什麼，但它讓我感覺很好。

Content: I just think that's an odd scene.
Content_zh: 我只是覺得這場戲很奇怪

Content: This is a nice soft picture of a lady that makes me feel pretty good.
Content_zh: 這張美麗的女士照片讓我感覺很好

Content: I don't know what these are gonna inspire at all, you guys.
Content_zh: 各位，我完全不知道這些會激發出什麼。

Content: I--I do all the drawings like kind of this just on pieces of paper like this.
Content_zh: 我……我所有這類圖都是畫在這種紙上。

Content: I just draw on Xerox paper and stuff, and I, like, I just scan 'em in and kind of make 'em
Content_zh: 我就畫在 Xerox 紙之類的東西上，然後掃進電腦，再把它們弄得

Content: look better later, pretty much, so.
Content_zh: 之後再讓它們看起來好一點，差不多就是這樣。

Content: It's easy for me to kind of like chill out in the morning in a café and do drawings like that,
Content_zh: 早上在咖啡館放鬆一下、畫這種圖，對我來說很容易，

Content: and then kind of like worry about, you know, just so I could kind of-- any way that I kind of relax
Content_zh: 然後再去煩別的事。只要能讓我放鬆、

Content: and kind of loosen up and stuff like that, and I find that if I'm not near a computer, I'm most relaxed.
Content_zh: 我發現如果我不在電腦附近 我最輕鬆了

Content: This is my little drawing zone.
Content_zh: 這是我的小畫區

Content_zh: 你看，我列印出很多我的 - 很多我的參考和 那種心情板。

Content: So I have this up and I'm constantly kind of changing it out.
Content_zh: 所以我把這些貼起來，也會一直更換。

Content: You know, update it, and with new things, make me feel different ways, so.
Content_zh: 不斷更新，換上新東西，讓我產生不同的感覺。

Content_zh: 就是這樣的

Content_zh: Bagel 和我是很要好的兄弟。

Content: I moved here, um, about four years ago, and he moved here like a month after.
Content_zh: 我大概四年前搬來這裡，他差不多一個月後也搬來。

Content_zh: 他當時住在我們另一個朋友那裡，後來就常在我家工作，因為我們還是在我家替 Double Fine 工作。

Content: And then he just was always over here, so he just found a place over here.

Content_zh: Bagel：這是我的臥室和工作室 我畫畫和睡覺的地方

Content: but that's about as far as I've gotten.

Content_zh: 但如果我想在牆上畫畫，我可以做到

Content_zh: 我就是工作、做案子，然後辦一次個展。

Content_zh: 過去大概三年都是這樣，所以我一年只需要煩一場大型展覽。

Content_zh: 像是他被沖上這座神秘島嶼，那是一座位在巨大湖泊中的魔法島，

Content_zh: 是的，我有個想法，這個洞穴人 被這恐龍解凍，最後，像，

Content: So I gotta like paint what's gonna be behind the arms, but he's gonna move it around, so you gotta see what's behind here.

Content: Uh, this game is gonna be so totally different and new, so who knows, you know?

Content: It's gonna be exciting when we start getting into like, uh, the real deal.

Content: I'm lookin' forward to that, it's gonna be awesome.
Content_zh: 我很期待，會很棒的

Content: Schafer: I've always loved Bagel's paintings.
Content_zh: Schafer：我一直很喜歡 Bagel 的畫。

Content: He's, um, when he does his fine art shows and galleries, he does these crazy works that are somewhat surreal,
Content_zh: 他辦純藝術展、在畫廊展出時，會做出一些很瘋狂、帶點超現實的作品，

Content: you know, playing with scale and playing with a mix of cartoony and, like, realism.
Content_zh: 會玩弄比例，也會混合卡通感和寫實感。

Content: He can do realistic stuff really well, mixes it with cartoony stuff and otherworldly type landscapes,
Content_zh: 他很擅長寫實的東西，又能和卡通感、異世界般的風景混在一起，

Content: and I think--I wanna do a whole game that'll look like those landscapes and with those strange characters.
Content_zh: 我想……我想做一整款看起來像那些風景、充滿那些奇怪角色的遊戲。

Content: In the end, all the art will go through Nathan, as he paints the final stuff.
Content_zh: 最後所有美術都會經過 Nathan，由他來畫最終稿。

Content: So it's all gonna look like his style, so it'll all be unified.
Content_zh: 所以所有東西都會呈現他的風格，也就會統一起來。

Content: You're just gonna see his take on the other artists' ideas and drawings.
Content_zh: 你會看到他對其他藝術家的想法和畫作的看法

Content: This'll be more than any game we've done before, like, uh, in the end, you know,
Content_zh: 這會比我們以前做過的任何遊戲都更像是，到了最後，

Content: a game where Bagel really gets to shine.
Content_zh: 一款能讓 Bagel 真正發光發熱的遊戲。

Content: Bagel: Yeah, I mean, I guess, I mean, and theoretically, he should be darker, like, you know?
Content_zh: Bagel：對，我是說，我想理論上他應該更暗一點，你懂嗎？

Content: I think he's too light right now.
Content_zh: 我覺得他現在太亮了。

Content: Like, he should blend--the colors, I think, should blend more with the environment.
Content_zh: 他應該更融入……我覺得顏色應該更融入環境。

Content: But, um, that's--that-- it's just not in yet.

Content: It's, uh...
Lee's gonna do some effects and aftereffects, I think, that lights the character's texture,

Content: so he would be a lot more blue and dark in this one.
Content_zh: 所以在這裡他會更偏藍、更暗。

Content: Nice.
Content_zh: 很好

Content: Killer.
Content_zh: 太殺了。

Content: I feel pretty awesome about it, I think.
Content_zh: 我覺得挺不錯的

Content: I feel great about everything.

Content: Uh, yeah, I think--it's--I'm, like, interested to see how it's gonna go, you know?
Content_zh: 嗯，對，我很想看看它最後會變成怎樣。

Content: Like I hope those guys don't hate my paintings of their paintings.
Content_zh: 我希望那些人不會討厭我把他們的畫重新畫成我的版本。

Content: You know, it's awesome 'cause they're just gonna be in a sweet painting world.
Content_zh: 這很棒，因為他們會進到一個超讚的繪畫世界裡。

Content: Like, that's-- that gets me pumped that people are gonna be able to, like, explore my paintings, you know?
Content_zh: 這點讓我很興奮，因為大家可以探索我的畫作，你懂嗎？

Content: Rice: That's hilarious.

Content: Bagel: That's amazing, though, dude. Rice: Oh, man, that's super good.

Content: Crook: So I'm actually scaling the arms a little bit so that it feels like they go-- Yeah, they get a little bit of perspective.
Content_zh: Crook：所以我其實把手臂稍微縮放了一下，讓它感覺像是……對，有一點透視感。

Content: And the feet. Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: 還有腳。Rice：對。

Content: Bagel: That totally, uh, that totally works.
Content_zh: Bagel：那完全……那完全可行。

Content: Petty: It's weird, it's a hybrid process 'cause it's like you're doing a 2D painting, but then we do have to get it on a 3D geometry,
Content_zh: Petty：這很奇妙，是一種混合流程，因為你像是在做 2D 繪圖，但我們又得把它放到 3D 幾何上，

Content: so it has some of the cleanup work as if it were a real 3D-- like a full 3D model.
Content_zh: 所以也有一些清理工作，像真的 3D，像完整 3D 模型那樣。

Content: Bagel: It depends on how complicated we're gonna get with all the parallaxing and, you know, I have never had to worry about memory issues
Content_zh: Bagel：這要看我們要把視差做得多複雜，而且我以前從來不用擔心記憶體問題

Content: in painting and restrictions, you know, technical restrictions or anything like that, so, yeah,
Content_zh: 畫畫時從來不用擔心限制，像技術限制之類的，所以，對，

Content: I mean, it's really, like, gonna be a crazy learning process at the same time, you know?
Content_zh: 我是說，這同時也會是很瘋狂的學習過程。

Content: Yeah, putting it all together and makin' it into a working, functional, moving world is, like, I haven't really done that before, so this is a whole--a whole new world.
Content_zh: 對，把這些全部組起來，變成一個能運作、有功能、會動的世界，我以前真的沒做過，所以這完全是個新世界。

Content: If I were to say, like, "Ah, here's this-- here's, like, him the most lit he's gonna be." Uh, you know, "Here's him dark as he's gonna be,"
Content_zh: 如果我說：「啊，這是他最亮時的樣子。」或是「這是他最暗時的樣子。」

Content: and then, like, you're--okay-- Petty: But I think it solves a different problem, right?
Content_zh: 然後你就……好吧。Petty：但我覺得那是在解決另一個問題，對吧？

Content: We're talking about-- like, I think the general color gradients, I think, look good.
Content_zh: 我們在說的是……我覺得整體的色彩漸層看起來不錯。

Content: I think the rim lighting is a different thing.

Content: That's not about lighting as much as it is, like, when there's an intense light source around.
Content_zh: 那與其說是一般打光，不如說是周圍有強烈光源時會出現的效果。

Content: It's about just putting a little bit on there to, like, tie him into the scene.
Content_zh: 只是稍微加一點，讓他融入場景。

Content: I think, I mean, you read the comments at the forum, and like everyone found it very appealing.
Content_zh: 我是說，你看論壇上的留言，大家都覺得很吸引人。

Content: It really helped the tie-in. Bagel: Yeah?
Content_zh: 那真的有助於把他融入場景。Bagel：是嗎？

Content: Schafer: Yeah, Bagel is not, um, really, you know, technical.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，Bagel 不是那麼技術取向的人。

Content: He likes to paint.
Content_zh: 他喜歡畫畫

Content: He likes to paint on, you know, canvas and paper, and, uh, um, and that's why it's really invaluable to have someone like Lee Petty on the team who can bridge that gap.
Content_zh: 他喜歡在畫布和紙上畫畫，所以團隊裡有 Lee Petty 這樣能銜接兩邊的人，真的非常寶貴。

Content: He's a fine artist himself, he can draw, he can paint, but he also knows how to use, um, incredibly advanced,
Content_zh: 他自己也是純藝術家，會畫圖、會繪畫，但他也懂得使用非常進階的

Content: you know, techniques and 3D rendering, and, you know, just making games that, you know, a lot of programmers, you know,
Content_zh: 那些技術和 3D 算圖，還有做遊戲這件事。很多程式設計師，

Content: he can match programmers sometimes with the knowledge of, um...
Content_zh: 有時候他的知識量甚至能跟程式設計師相比……

Content: He's got a lot of knowledge. That's what I'm trying to say.
Content_zh: 他懂很多。這就是我想說的。

Content: So it's great to have someone like Lee around who can bridge that gap.
Content_zh: 所以身邊有 Lee 這樣能銜接落差的人真的很棒。

Content: Petty: That's why I was saying earlier, don't paint the character dark because you can never get lighter.
Content_zh: Petty：所以我剛才才說，不要把角色畫得太暗，因為之後就很難再變亮。

Content: We want to generally paint him pretty light like this 'cause then he can get darker.
Content_zh: 我們通常會想把他畫得像這樣偏亮，因為之後還可以變暗。

Content: Schafer: If you can span both worlds, it's really essential, 'cause you can work with a great artist, but then if that doesn't really translate into the game,
Content_zh: Schafer：如果你能橫跨兩個世界，那真的很重要。因為你可以和很棒的藝術家合作，但如果那些東西沒辦法真正轉進遊戲裡，

Content: you can be, um, it's just a waste.
Content_zh: 那就只是浪費而已。

Content: Chan: Yeah, I'm--I'm curious as much as you are, and I have faith that it'll work.

Content: Perfect. I think that's a great idea.

Content: And so I trust him.

Content: If that's what he wants, then-- then there's something there.
